issues.description: SEC-4.2.4 (data/sections.json:part4.html, details id=p4-424) presents per-sub-test analysis for E-PRED-A, B, C, and D as if all four are currently labeled on the live dome predictions page. As of 2026-05-07, only E-PRED-B ("E-PRED-B LOCKED") is explicitly labeled; A, C, and D are folded into the Tier 1/2/3 κ-coupling protocol without explicit E-PRED labels. EXP-292 patched the parallel occurrence in SEC-8b.3; this issue tracks the analogous fix needed in SEC-4.2.4: anchor each sub-test's label-presence claim to the 2026-03-12 snapshot date and note current live-page status. Flagged by EXP-292 parallel_issue_flag field. Additionally, SEC-4.2.4's TLDR mischaracterizes E-PRED-C as "(E-PRED-C, timing lag)" when E-PRED-C is actually "SG gravimeters 0.0 µGal" — a null gravity prediction, not a timing lag. TLDR factual error severity: major (readers see TLDRs first). The TLDR text should read "(E-PRED-C, gravity null)" or "(E-PRED-C, SG gravimeter null)". Body prose is correct; only TLDR is wrong. Flagged by curmudgeon review SEC-8b.3.c8.json (hole 3), reviewed_at 2026-05-07T18:44:26.580Z.
issues.fix_details: TLDR sub-issue FIXED 2026-05-08 via direct FUSE edit (bash unavailable, disk full): replaced '(E-PRED-C, timing lag)' with '(E-PRED-C, gravity null)' in data/sections.json. workspace-sync will push within 1h; HTML rebuild needed on next successful decider run. REMAINING: the per-sub-test E-PRED label-presence snapshot anchor fix (the EXP-292 analogy) is OPEN — requires analyst EXP for the fuller prose rework of SEC-4.2.4 body. Keeping issue open to track remaining body-prose work.
issues.tldr_fix_verification_pattern: git show origin/main:data/sections.json | grep -qF '(E-PRED-C, gravity null)'
issues.recommended_action: EXP: Patch SEC-4.2.4 (part4.html, p4-424) analogously to EXP-292: preserve per-sub-test analysis but anchor each E-PRED label-presence claim to the 2026-03-12 snapshot, and note that as of 2026-05-07 only E-PRED-B is explicitly labeled on the live page (A, C, D folded into Tier 1/2/3 protocol).
issues.severity_upgraded_by: decider — curmudgeon review SEC-8b.3.c8.json hole 3: TLDR factual error; per curmudgeon.md TLDR factual errors are MAJOR

issues.title: WIN-014 c11 H1+H5: coverage geography precision (in-corridor site naming, city-tier split) — self-applied
issues.description: H1 (moderate): New WIN-014 prose said gravimeters were not necessarily uniformly on the totality path but never named a confirmed in-corridor site, making the network-wide null appear to involve only off-path stations. H5 (minor): Beijing, Changchun, Lhasa, Urumqi lumped as same coverage tier. Fixed: added Wuhan as in-corridor full-totality SG site; split cities into mid-latitude (Beijing ~75%, Changchun ~70%) vs lower-coverage (Lhasa ~50-60%, Urumqi <40%) tiers.
issues.resolution_summary: Patched via self-apply: named Wuhan as in-corridor totality site; split Beijing/Changchun (~70-75%) from Lhasa (~50-60%) and Urumqi (<40%) into coverage tiers.

issues.title: WIN-014 c11 H2: 70% coverage anchor needs low-end bracket — self-applied
issues.description: H2 (moderate): The 70% coverage anchor was rhetorically convenient but not motivated by the actual distribution. Lhasa and Urumqi were substantially below 70%. The arithmetic only showed the 70% case. Fixed: added parenthetical showing the 50% coverage case (Δg 1.5-4.5 µGal, still 1.5×-45× above floor) and noted only sites below ~30% fall to floor.
issues.resolution_summary: Patched: added 50% low-end coverage bracket and noted ~30% floor threshold.

issues.title: WIN-014 c11 H3+H4: quadratic scaling missing, vise over-confident — self-applied
issues.description: H3 (minor): "any plausible coverage scaling" asserted robustness but prose only showed linear case; quadratic (0.7²≈0.49) from EXP-349 omitted. H4 (minor): Kim & Chang vise used "consistent with... also exempts much of" (over-confident, unanchored in observatory-distance data). Fixed: added quadratic parenthetical; hedged vise to "high enough to exempt... would also exclude a substantial fraction".
issues.resolution_summary: Patched: added quadratic scaling parenthetical; hedged Kim & Chang vise language.

issues.title: build-scripts/generate-html.js references WIN-054 as Not-Demonstrated example, but WIN-054 is now SC
issues.description: build-scripts/generate-html.js line ~1726 contains: "Pure false-dilemma — correctly identifying a genuine anomaly without offering dome physics — is Not Demonstrated rather than Misleading; WIN-054 (El Gordo) is the reference case for this distinction." WIN-054 was reclassified to Self-Contradicted in c3/EXP-295. The reference case is now stale and would confuse readers who cross-check.
issues.suggested_fix: Replace WIN-054 reference with a current Not-Demonstrated WIN (e.g. WIN-011 Mohe 1997 gravity anomaly) or drop the specific WIN citation and let the category description stand on its own.
issues.notes: REOPENED 2026-05-25 by operator: prior closure was FALSE — fix_description claims WIN-054→WIN-011 replacement was applied but generate-html.js line ~1726 STILL contains 'WIN-054 (El Gordo) is the reference case' on both FUSE and git (verified by tinker run 2026-05-25T02-48 FND-01). See HNOTE-OPERATOR-ISS-2094-FALSE-CLOSURE-001 for full diagnostic. Stranded for operator: NEVER_PUSH file. See stranded-patches file for exact find/replace.

ISS-2126 (closed 2026-05-19T05:20Z) flagged this at c6 with 2 suggested fixes; only fix (a) git rm new-wins file was applied. The load-bearing fix (b) — decider dedup check before win-new push — was NOT implemented. Curmudgeon escalated to MAJOR in c7 review (2026-05-19T10:44Z) with verdict_holds undefined but explicit operational concern. Each loop iteration burns ~1 decider-Opus run + ~1 curmudgeon-Opus run on byte-identical work. Predecessor: ISS-2126.
issues.notes: Curmudgeon recommended decider patch: in the win-new push handler, before pushing target_type:win-new to priority-queue.json, check (1) does data/wins.json already contain a WIN entry with this id? AND (2) does monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ already contain a WIN-{id}.c{n}.json file? If BOTH true, do NOT push — log "WIN-{id} already integrated and reviewed; no queue push needed" and continue. Routed to tinker for structural fix design (DIRECTIVE-20260519-002 filed alongside).
issues.assigned_tinker_reason: Structural decider-prompt patch — needs design + counter-arguments

issues.title: WIN-021.c4 H1 (minor): t² coefficient factor-of-2 elision in P03 angular acceleration paragraph
issues.description: WIN-021 detail_evidence says the t² coefficient (≈1 arcsec/century²) converts to roughly 10⁻²⁴ rad/s², but silently conflates polynomial coefficient (5.25×10⁻²⁵) with actual angular acceleration (1.05×10⁻²⁴, which is 2× the coefficient). Both round to 10⁻²⁴ and "twelve orders of magnitude" holds either way, but a physicist could note the missing factor-of-2. Suggested fix: replace "the t² coefficient of the longitudinal expression (≈1 arcsec/century²), which converts to roughly 10⁻²⁴ rad/s²" with "the second derivative of the longitudinal expansion (twice the t² coefficient of ≈1 arcsec/century², i.e. ≈2 arcsec/century²), which converts to ≈10⁻²⁴ rad/s²".
issues.fix_notes: DEGRADED-MODE FUSE APPLY: bash unavailable. Applied exact replacement from ISS description. Tests NOT run.

issues.title: WIN-021.c4 H2 (minor): missing inline citation to Capitaine et al. 2003 A&A 412, 567 for P03 model
issues.description: The new P03-model text in WIN-021 detail_evidence lacks an inline link to the Capitaine, Wallace, Chapront (2003) A&A 412, 567 paper (DOI: 10.1051/0004-6361:20031539), which is the canonical source for the P03 longitudinal expansion coefficients cited. The IAU 2006 Resolution B1 PDF link covers the angular velocity but not the t² coefficient machinery. Suggested fix: add IAU 2006 P03 model next to the first mention of "IAU 2006 P_03 model".
issues.fix_notes: DEGRADED-MODE FUSE APPLY: bash unavailable. Added Capitaine et al. 2003 DOI link around 'IAU 2006 P_03 model' text. Tests NOT run.

issues.title: WIN-012 c9 H1+H3 (moderate): P3 paragraph precision — 'labels the same −10.9 nT excursion' is rebuttable
issues.description: WIN-012 P3 header reads 'The dome's own labels of the same number disagree' and claims the Forensic Zoom chart 'labels the same −10.9 nT excursion as the October 2003 G5 Halloween storm.' Per curmudgeon c9 Hole 1 (moderate): the chart shows ±20-23 nT excursions from the storm, NOT a labeled −10.9 nT point. The chart argues for the same κ = 1.677 constant via r = 0.992 correlation — it does not independently re-derive the same −10.9 nT numerator. The 'dome cannot have it both ways / If the numerator is X / If the numerator is Y' false-dilemma framing is rebuttable (dome defender correctly says: 'eclipse defines κ, storm validates it via correlation'). H3 (minor, folded into H1 patch): 'numerical agreement is parameter-fit residue' is ambiguous — replaced with 'the reuse of κ across distinct physical regimes is unanchored to dome geometry.' SELF-APPLIED 2026-05-21: P3 rewritten to 'The dome applies κ across physically distinct regimes without a derivation' framing with count-contradiction (2 vs 3 events per raw-text/02-wins.txt:87 vs WIN-058b and WIN-070). Curmudgeon c10 re-verification queued (qid 395, class=verification).
issues.fix_notes: DEGRADED-MODE FUSE APPLY: bash unavailable (disk full). Applied directly to FUSE wins.json. workspace-sync will rescue to git when restored. Tests NOT run (bash unavailable). Patch follows curmudgeon c9 H1 recommendation exactly.

issues.title: WIN-012 c9 H2 (moderate): `finding` field lags detail_evidence — κ-as-bridge vise invisible at entry-point
issues.description: WIN-012 `finding` field still carries the c1-era surface-contradiction framing ('6.5 µGal eclipse gravity anomaly to derive κ = 1.67'). After EXP-430, the centerpiece attack is the dome-internal-labels inconsistency and the κ-as-bridge structural vise. Most readers see `finding` before deciding to expand. Per curmudgeon c9 Hole 2 (moderate): update finding to surface (1) κ defined from two post-hoc measurements, (2) denominator vanishes per WIN-013/014, (3) dome applies κ across two physically distinct event types (eclipse 2017, storm 2003) without disc-geometry derivation. SELF-APPLIED 2026-05-21: finding rewritten per curmudgeon recommended text. Curmudgeon c10 re-verification queued (qid 395, class=verification).
issues.fix_notes: DEGRADED-MODE FUSE APPLY: bash unavailable (disk full). Applied directly to FUSE wins.json. workspace-sync will rescue to git when restored. Tests NOT run (bash unavailable).

issues.title: PRED-073 uncatalogued: SAA western cell drift ≥0.3° by May 2026 — imminent window (~8 days)
issues.description: Poller chg-20260523-0703-002 discovered PRED-073 (SAA Western Cell Drift ≥0.3° Westward by May 2026) on dome predictions page but absent from our predictions.json catalog. Test window closes end-of-May 2026 (≈2026-05-31). Dome status: PENDING PROSPECTIVE. SHA256: 291f2697... Prediction registered 2026-04-04. Dome claims: ECM predicts SAA western cell drifts ≥0.3° further westward April→May 2026 consistent with toroidal aetheric return flow. Falsification rule: SAA western cell drifts <0.1° westward or reverses direction. Data source per dome: CHAOS-7/NOAA NGDC monthly maps (typically available early June). Action: analyst should (1) add PRED-073 to predictions.json with category=geomagnetic, prospective=true, our_verdict=null, test_window_closes=2026-05-31; (2) assess whether May 2026 CHAOS-7 data available yet; (3) monitor dome for result post before end of month.
issues.resolution: Decider added PRED-073 to predictions.json directly (time-sensitive: window closes 2026-05-31). Entry includes our_verdict=pending, testability=medium, with analyst note to complete assessment.
